scope.daily_notes_status: blocked (sqlite db unreadable from Linux FUSE sandbox)

summary: The listener spent the week getting the first full set of head-to-head ballot pairings: Luedtke vs Hidayat in Council D7, Jawando vs Friedson on the Executive seat, and Vaughn Stewart's record next to the seven-candidate D19 House field. Sanctuary policy was the most-returned-to listener priority across all five candidates — every Episode 3 or 4 of the week contained an explicit on-air, named answer for that candidate. Friedson's fiscal record, Wootton, and Ben Kramer's career-long voting record were referenced but NOT yet broken down line-item — they are tomorrow's promised work.
themes: Sanctuary / anti-detention as a sorting axis | Stewart was situated as the strongest pro-sanctuary candidate on the listener's ballot (Dignity Not Detention 2021 lead sponsor); Glass as pro-sanctuary via 2021 Trust Act / Ethnic & Civic Activities support; Luedtke heavy sanctuary-protection record; Hidayat the only on-ballot D7 contrast — show kept its promise to give each candidate an explicit yes/no on the listener's deciding question.

Up-county vs down-county budget equity | Hidayat ep4 turned the D7 race on Thrive Montgomery 2050 (he opposed, Luedtke voted yes Oct 2022) — the cleanest documented Luedtke-Hidayat split. Worth referencing once when Van Free comes up (also D7).

MD-8/Raskin connection as a credentialing line | Stewart ep2 surfaced his 2015 role as policy director on Jamie Raskin's MD-8 primary; this is a recurring legitimization frame the show should not over-rely on for other candidates with weaker links.
Hidayat's 2022 fifth-place finish + 'red-headed stepchildren' quote | Ep3 used this as his through-line. Safe to reference, but don't re-use as a fresh hook in any other candidate's episode.
Glass's 'run the buses, teach the kids, pick up the trash' as personal tagline | Used in ep1, ep2, ep4 of Glass week. Already on the SKILL.md avoid-list. Confirming as off-limits.
open_questions: Wootton High School closure timeline — who votes, when, procedural path. Touched in Glass ep4 framing (school accountability) but the actual Board-of-Education vote calendar has not been laid out for the listener on-air yet.

Andrew Friedson's line-item fiscal record — Glass ep4 mentioned that Glass wants to add a CE-appointed BoE seat for accountability, but did NOT do a side-by-side of Friedson's individual MCPS budget line votes versus the other candidates. Still owed.
Ben Kramer's full historical voting record back to 2007 on consumer protection / judiciary / immigration / education funding. Kramer's series is scheduled but the deep historical pull hasn't aired.

Sanctuary side-by-side matrix across the full Exec field — we've answered Stewart, Glass, Luedtke, Hidayat, Jawando individually, but a one-screen matrix that puts all five Execs on the same axis is not yet on air.
New MCPS rating methodology and how each Rockville school is graded under it — touched in passing during Glass ep4 but not explained.
underserved_topics: Wootton High School closure procedural path (mentioned, never laid out).
Friedson line-item budget votes vs. final-budget yes/no.
Kramer career-long votes (the candidate's own series will reach this, but a preview matrix would be useful sooner).
Side-by-side ballot matrix as a standalone summary deliverable — the listener has asked for this repeatedly; the series-week format keeps deferring it.
The five tier-3/tier-4 races finalized 2026-05-16 (State's Attorney, Clerk, Register of Wills, Sheriff, Sixth Judicial Circuit judges) — listener has had zero coverage of any of these yet.
avoid_list: 'Thirty-X days out from the Maryland Democratic primary' cold-open device — used 5/16, 5/17, 5/18, 5/19 ep1s. Officially exhausted; pick a new opener.
'Montgomery County native' as a framing hook — leaned on 5/16 Friedson ep1, revisited 5/18 Hidayat ep1, and 5/19 Jawando ep1. Stop.
'My Seven Black Fathers' memoir reference — Jawando-only (5/19).
'Anniston, Alabama / Donoho School / Vaughn Stewart Junior as mayor' — Vaughn Stewart-only (5/20).
'Born in Trenton, between Atlantic City and Philadelphia / adopted as an infant from a teenage mom in a convent' — Luedtke-only (5/17).
'First Muslim officer of the Montgomery County Police Department' — Hidayat-only (5/18).
'Westbury, Long Island / CNN bureau library / Gandhi Brigade Youth Media' — Evan Glass-only (5/21).
'Run the buses, teach the kids, pick up the trash' — Glass tagline only (5/21).

'ICE Out Act / Trust Act / DHS officer screening bill' as the immigration-record framing HOOK — used heavily in Glass 5/21 ep3 and ep4. Can still be referenced for comparison, but it should not LEAD-FRAME any candidate's immigration record next week.
'Dignity Not Detention Act of 2021' as the lead-framing of any candidate other than Vaughn Stewart — he is the sponsor, others can only be co-sponsors.
'Pre-political biography is thinner in the public record / no Wikipedia entry adds independent verification' as a reusable candidate-specific qualifier — 5/16 Friedson ep1. Stop.
'Thrive Montgomery 2050' as a Council District 7 sorting axis — fully discharged on 5/18 ep4. Can be referenced in passing for D7 candidates only; do not re-use as a head-to-head device elsewhere.
Three-bill arc framing ('Law Enforcement Trust 2019 → Use-of-Force 2020 → Community Informed Training 2021') — Jawando-only (5/19 ep3).
note_count_unavailable_reason: daily_notes DB unreadable from Linux sandbox; podcast_*.txt files were used as the secondary signal source per the instructions step 2 fallback.
